title: Genome-wide Identification of DNA Methylation Biomarkers in AML

title: Biomarkers of Sulforaphane/Broccoli Sprout Extract in Prostate Cancer

key_phrases: Determine whether daily oral BroccoMaxÂ® administration for 4 weeks decreases circulating and prostate tumor levels of fatty acids through a pilot double-blind, randomized, and placebo-controlled window of opportunity trial in men scheduled for prostatectomy.

The preclinical studies are conceived to test the hypothesis that prostate cancer chemoprevention by SFN and BSE in a clinically-relevant transgenic mouse model (Hi-Myc) is associated with suppression of synthesis as well as Î²-oxidation of fatty acids leading to inhibition of cancer cell proliferation.

Specifically, we found that SFN treatment not only suppresses protein/mRNA levels of ACC1 and FASN as well as the dehydrogenases implicated in Î²-oxidation of fatty acids but also decreases acetyl-CoA levels in human prostate cancer cells in vitro and prostate tumors of TRAMP mice in vivo.
classification.reasoning: The study aims to identify biomarkers that show the effect of sulforaphane on fatty acid metabolism, making it a pharmacodynamic biomarker. It also aims to use this for chemoprevention of prostate cancer, which is a susceptibility risk. title: The role of oxidative stress and inflammation in epileptogenesis

title: Phase II Clinical trial of GTC in Men on Active Surveillance

key_phrases: We hypothesize that men with biopsy proven adenocarcinoma of the prostate who meet the criteria for AS, who receive GTC at a dose of 800 mg EGCG per day (vs. placebo) for 24 months, will have a significantly decreased rate of clinical progression.

We will test this hypothesis using a rigorous experimental design in a phase II, randomized clinical trial to evaluate the safety, effectiveness and potential mechanism by which GTC modulates clinical progression and the related biological biomarkers relevant to PCa progression in men on AS for PCa.

Several epidemiological, in vitro, preclinical and early phase trials completed by our team and others have shown that the GTC are potent inhibitors of PCa carcinogenesis through multiple mechanisms, bioavailable in tissue and plasma, reduces several intermediate biomarkers implicated in PCa progression and without toxicities at these doses.
classification.reasoning: The study is a randomized clinical trial evaluating the effectiveness of GTC in reducing the rate of clinical progression in men on active surveillance for prostate cancer. The evidence strength is experimental_weak because the biomarkers are being measured within an RCT but not validated as surrogate endpoints or shown to mediate the treatment effect.
